usize,\n    visited: &mut Vec<Ty<'tcx>>,\n    is_reference_target: bool,\n) -> TypeTree {\n    if ty.is_scalar() {\n        let (kind, size) = if ty.is_integral() || ty.is_char() || ty.is_bool() {\n            (Kind::Integer, ty.primitive_size(tcx).bytes_usize())\n        } else if ty.is_floating_point() {\n            match ty {\n                x if x == tcx.types.f16 => (Kind::Half, 2),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f32 => (Kind::Float, 4),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f64 => (Kind::Double, 8),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f128 => (Kind::F128, 16),\n                _ => (Kind::Integer, 0),\n            }\n        } else {\n            (Kind::Integer, 0)\n        };\n\n        // Use offset 0 for scalars that are direct targets of references (like &f64)\n        // Use offset -1 for scalars used directly (like function return types)\n        let offset = if is_reference_target && !ty.is_array() { 0 } else { -1 };\n        return TypeTree(vec![Type { offset, size, kind, child: TypeTree::new() }]);\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_ref() || ty.is_raw_ptr() || ty.is_box() {\n        let inner_ty = if let Some(inner) = ty.builtin_deref(true) {\n            inner\n        } else {\n            return TypeTree::new();\n        };\n\n        let child = typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, inner_ty, depth + 1, visited, true);\n        return TypeTree(vec![Type {\n            offset: -1,\n            size: tcx.data_layout.pointer_size().bytes_usize(),\n            kind: Kind::Pointer,\n            child,\n        }]);\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_array() {\n        if let ty::Array(element_ty, len_const) = ty.kind() {\n            let len = len_const.try_to_target_usize(tcx).unwrap_or(0);\n            if len == 0 {\n                return TypeTree::new();\n            }\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, *element_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n            let mut types = Vec::new();\n            for elem_type in &element_tree.0 {\n                types.push(Type {\n                    offset: -1,\n                    size: elem_type.size,\n                    kind: elem_type.kind,\n                    child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n\n            return TypeTree(types);\n        }\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_slice() {\n        if let ty::Slice(element_ty) = ty.kind() {\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, *element_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n            return element_tree;\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let ty::Tuple(tuple_types) = ty.kind() {\n        if tuple_types.is_empty() {\n            return TypeTree::new();\n        }\n\n        let mut types = Vec::new();\n        let mut current_offset = 0;\n\n        for tuple_ty in tuple_types.iter() {\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, tuple_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n\n            let element_layout = tcx\n                .layout_of(ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized().as_query_input(tuple_ty))\n                .ok()\n                .map(|layout| layout.size.bytes_usize())\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n\n            for elem_type in &element_tree.0 {\n                types.push(Type {\n                    offset: if elem_type.offset == -1 {\n                        current_offset as isize\n                    } else {\n                        current_offset as isize + elem_type.offset\n                    },\n                    size: elem_type.size,\n                    kind: elem_type.kind,\n                    child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n\n            current_offset += element_layout;\n        }\n\n        return TypeTree(types);\n    }\n\n    if let ty::Adt(adt_def, args) = ty.kind() {\n        if adt_def.is_struct() {\n            let struct_layout =\n                tcx.layout_of(ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized().as_query_input(ty));\n            if let Ok(layout) = struct_layout {\n                let mut types = Vec::new();\n\n                for (field_idx, field_def) in adt_def.all_fields().enumerate() {\n                    let field_ty = field_def.ty(tcx, args);\n                    let field_tree =\n                        typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, field_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n\n                    let field_offset = layout.fields.offset(field_idx).bytes_usize();\n\n                    for elem_type in &field_tree.0 {\n                        types.push(Type {\n                            offset: if elem_type.offset == -1 {\n                                field_offset as isize\n                            } else {\n                                field_offset as isize + elem_type.offset\n                            },\n                            size: elem_type.size,\n                            kind: elem_type.kind,\n                            child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                return TypeTree(types);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    TypeTree::new()\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":91,"oldLines":7,"newStart":91,"newLines":7,"lines":["     PolyExistentialPredicate, PolyExistentialProjection, I: Interner)]\n#[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic)]\n#[cfg_attr(\n    feature = \"nightly\",\n    derive(Encodable_NoContext, Decodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n)]\npub enum ClauseKind<I: Interner> {\n    /// Corresponds to `where Foo: Bar<A, B, C>`. `Foo` here would be\n    /// the `Self` type of the trait reference and `A`, `B`, and `C`\n    /// would be the type parameters.\n    Trait(ty::TraitPredicate<I>),\n\n    /// `where 'a: 'r`\n    RegionOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate<I, I::Region>),\n\n    /// `where T: 'r`\n    TypeOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate<I, I::Ty>),\n\n    /// `where <T as TraitRef>::Name == X`, approximately.\n    /// See the `ProjectionPredicate` struct for details.\n    Projection(ty::ProjectionPredicate<I>),\n\n    /// Ensures that a const generic argument to a parameter `const N: u8`\n    /// is of type `u8`.\n    ConstArgHasType(I::Const, I::Ty),\n\n    /// No syntax: `T` well-formed.\n    WellFormed(I::Term),\n\n    /// Constant initializer must evaluate successfully.\n    ConstEvaluatable(I::Const),\n\n    /// Enforces the constness of the predicate we're calling. At the moment, we actually process coercions\n    /// very much like subtyping and don't handle the full coercion\n    /// logic.\n    Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate<I>),\n\n    /// Constants must be equal. The first component is the const that is expected.\n    ConstEquate(I::Const, I::Const),\n\n    /// A marker predicate that is always ambiguous.\n    /// Used for coherence to mark opaque types as possibly equal to each other but ambiguous.\n    Ambiguous,\n\n    /// This should only be used inside of the new solver for `AliasRelate` and expects\n    /// the `term` to be always be an unconstrained inference variable. It is used to\n    /// normalize `alias` as much as possible. In case the alias is rigid - i.e. it cannot\n    /// be normalized in the current environment - this constrains `term` to be equal to\n    /// the alias itself.\n    ///\n    /// It is likely more AliasRelate(I::Term, I::Term, AliasRelationDirection),\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> Eq for PredicateKind<I> {}\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug, Copy)]\n#[cfg_attr(\n Imagine you have a function `F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R`, plus an iterator `iter`\n/// that produces `T` items. You could combine them with\n/// `f(&iter.collect::<Vec<_>>())`, but this requires allocating memory for the\n/// `Vec`.\n///\n/// This trait allows for faster implementations, intended for cases where the\n/// number of items produced by the iterator is small. There is a blanket impl\n/// for `T` items, but there is also a fallible impl for `Result<T, E>` items.\npub trait CollectAndApply<T, R>: Sized {\n    type Output;\n\n    /// Produce a result of type `Self::Output` from `iter`. The result will\n    /// typically be produced by applying `f` on the elements produced by\n    /// `iter`, though this may not happen in some impls, e.g. if an error\n    /// occurred during iteration.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(iter: I, f: F) -> Self::Output\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = Self>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R;\n}\n\n/// The blanket impl that always collects all elements and applies `f`.\nimpl<T, R> CollectAndApply<T, R> for T {\n    type Output = R;\n\n    /// Equivalent to `f(&iter.collect::<Vec<_>>())`.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(mut iter: I, f: F) -> R\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = T>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R,\n    {\n        // This code is hot enough that it's worth specializing for the most\n        // common length lists, to avoid the overhead of `Vec` creation.\n\n        let Some(t0) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t1) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t2) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t3) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t4) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t5) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t6) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t7) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t8) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7]);\n        };\n\n        f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7, t8].into_iter().chain(iter).collect::<Vec<_>>())\n    }\n}\n\n/// A fallible impl that will fail, without calling `f`, if there are any\n/// errors during collection.\nimpl<T, R, E> CollectAndApply<T, R> for Result<T, E> {\n    type Output = Result<R, E>;\n\n    /// Equivalent to `Ok(f(&iter.collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?))`.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(mut iter: I, f: F) -> Result<R, E>\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = Result<T, E>>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R,\n    {\n        // This code is hot enough that it's worth specializing for the most\n        // common length lists, to avoid the overhead of `Vec` creation.\n\n        let Some(t0) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[]));\n        };\n        let t0 = t0?;\n\n        let Some(t1) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0]));\n        };\n        let t1 = t1?;\n\n        let Some(t2) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1]));\n        };\n        let t2 = t2?;\n\n        let Some(t3) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2]));\n        };\n        let t3 = t3?;\n\n        let Some(t4) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3]));\n        };\n        let t4 = t4?;\n\n        let Some(t5) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4]));\n        };\n        let t5 = t5?;\n\n        let Some(t6) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5]));\n        };\n        let t6 = t6?;\n\n        let Some(t7) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6]));\n        };\n        let t7 = t7?;\n\n        let Some(t8) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7]));\n        };\n        let t8 = t8?;\n\n        Ok(f(&[Ok(t0), Ok(t1), Ok(t2), Ok(t3), Ok(t4), Ok(t5), Ok(t6), Ok(t7), Ok(t8)]\n            .into_iter()\n            .chain(iter)\n            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> search_graph::Cx for I {\n    type Input = CanonicalInput<I>;\n    type Result = QueryResult<I>;\n    type AmbiguityInfo = Certainty;\n\n    type DepNodeIndex = I::DepNodeIndex;\n    type Tracked<T: Debug + Clone> = I::Tracked<T>;\n    fn mk_tracked<T: Debug + Clone>(\n        self,\n        data: T,\n        dep_node_index: I::DepNodeIndex,\n    ) -> I::Tracked<T> {\n        I::mk_tracked(self, data, dep_node_index)\n    }\n    fn get_tracked<T: Debug + Clone>(self, tracked: &I::Tracked<T>) -> T {\n        I::get_tracked(self, tracked)\n    }\n    fn with_cached_task<T>(self, task: impl FnOnce() -> T) -> (T, I::DepNodeIndex) {\n        I::with_cached_task(self, task)\n    }\n    fn with_global_cache<R>(self, f: impl FnOnce(&mut search_graph::GlobalCache<Self>) -> R) -> R {\n        I::with_global_cache(self, f)\n    }\n    fn assert_evaluation_is_concurrent(&self) {\n        self.assert_evaluation_is_concurrent()\n These flags are propagated upwards\n    /// through the type during type construction, so that we can quickly check\n    /// whether the type has various kinds of types in it without recursing\n    /// over the type itself.\n    #[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone, Copy)]\n    pub struct TypeFlags: u32 {\n        // Does this have parameters? Used to determine whether instantiation is\n        // required.\n        /// Does this have `Param`?\n        const HAS_TY_PARAM                = 1 << 0;\n        /// Does this have `ReEarlyParam`?\n        const HAS_RE_PARAM                = 1 << 1;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Param`?\n        const HAS_CT_PARAM                = 1 << 2;\n\n        const HAS_PARAM                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Infer`?\n        const HAS_TY_INFER                = 1 << 3;\n        /// Does this have `ReVar`?\n        const HAS_RE_INFER                = 1 << 4;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Infer`?\n        const HAS_CT_INFER                = 1 << 5;\n\n        /// Does this have inference variables? Used to determine whether\n        /// inference is required.\n        const HAS_INFER                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Placeholder`?\n        const HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 6;\n        /// Does this have `RePlaceholder`?\n        const HAS_RE_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 7;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Placeholder`?\n        const HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 8;\n\n        /// Does this have placeholders?\n        const HAS_PLACEHOLDER             = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits();\n\n        /// `true` if there are \"names\" of regions and so forth\n        /// that are local to a particular fn/inferctxt\n        const HAS_FREE_LOCAL_REGIONS      = 1 << 9;\n\n        /// `true` if there are \"names\" of types and regions and so forth\n        /// that are local to a particular fn\n        const HAS_FREE_LOCAL_NAMES        = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          // We consider 'freshened' types and constants\n                                          // to depend on a particular fn.\n                                          // The freshening process throws away information,\n                                          // which can make things unsuitable for use in a global\n                                          // cache. Note that there is no 'fresh lifetime' flag -\n                                          // freshening replaces all lifetimes with `ReErased`,\n                                          // which is different from how types/const are freshened.\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FRESH.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_FRESH.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_FREE_LOCAL_REGIONS.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_ERASED.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Projection`?\n        const HAS_TY_PROJECTION           = 1 << 10;\n        /// Does this have `Free` aliases?\n        const HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS                 = 1 << 11;\n        /// Does this have `Opaque`?\n        const HAS_TY_OPAQUE               = 1 << 12;\n        /// Does this have `Inherent`?\n        const HAS_TY_INHERENT             = 1 << 13;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Unevaluated`?\n        const HAS_CT_PROJECTION           = 1 << 14;\n\n        /// Does this have `Alias` or `ConstKind::Unevaluated`?\n        ///\n        /// Rephrased, could this term be normalized further?\n        const HAS_ALIAS                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PROJECTION.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_OPAQUE.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INHERENT.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PROJECTION.bits();\n\n        /// Is an error type/lifetime/const reachable?\n        const HAS_ERROR                   = 1 << 15;\n\n        /// Does this have any region that \"appears free\" in the type?\n        /// Basically anything but `ReBound` and `ReErased`.\n        const HAS_FREE_REGIONS            = 1 << 16;\n\n        /// Does this have any `ReBound` regions?\n        const HAS_RE_BOUND                = 1 << 17;\n        /// Does this have any `Bound` types?\n        const HAS_TY_BOUND                = 1 << 18;\n        /// Does this have any `ConstKind::Bound` consts?\n        const HAS_CT_BOUND                = 1 << 19;\n        /// Does this have any bound variables?\n        /// Used to check if a global bound is safe to evaluate.\n        const HAS_BOUND_VARS              = TypeFlags::HAS_RE_BOUND.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have any `ReErased` regions?\n        const HAS_RE_ERASED               = 1 << 20;\n\n        /// Does this value have parameters/placeholders/inference variables which could be\n        /// replaced later, in a way that would change the results of `impl` specialization?\n        const STILL_FURTHER_SPECIALIZABLE = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits();\n\n        /// Does this value have `InferTy::FreshTy/FreshIntTy/FreshFloatTy`?\n        const HAS_TY_FRESH                = 1 << 21;\n\n        /// Does this value have `InferConst::Fresh`?\n        const HAS_CT_FRESH                = 1 << 22;\n\n        /// Does this have any binders with bound vars (e.g. that need to be anonymized)?\n        const HAS_BINDER_VARS             = 1 << 23;\n\n        /// Does this type have any coroutines in it?\n        const HAS_TY_CORO                 = 1 << 24;\n\n        /// Does this have have a `Bound(BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _)`?\n        const HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND      = 1 << 25;\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct FlagComputation<I> {\n    pub flags: TypeFlags,\n\n    /// see `Ty::outer_exclusive_binder` for details\n    pub outer_exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex,\n\n    interner: std::marker::PhantomData<I>,\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> FlagComputation<I> {\n    fn new() -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        FlagComputation {\n            flags: TypeFlags::empty(),\n            outer_exclusive_binder: ty::INNERMOST,\n            interner: std::marker::PhantomData,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    pub fn for_kind(kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_predicate(binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_predicate(binder);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_const_kind(kind: &ty::ConstKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_const_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_clauses(clauses: &[I::Clause]) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        for c in clauses {\n            result.add_flags(c.as_predicate().flags());\n            result.add_exclusive_binder(c.as_predicate().outer_exclusive_binder());\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn add_flags(&mut self, flags: TypeFlags) {\n        self.flags = self.flags | flags;\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something at binding level `binder`\n    fn add_bound_var(&mut self, binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        let exclusive_binder = binder.shifted_in(1);\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something *inside* binding\n    /// level `binder` -- not bound by `binder`, but bound by the next\n    /// binder internal to it\n    fn add_exclusive_binder(&mut self, exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        self.outer_exclusive_binder = self.outer_exclusive_binder.max(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// Adds the flags/depth from a set of types that appear within the current type, but within a\n    /// region binder.\n    fn bound_computation<T, F>(&mut self, value: ty::Binder<I, T>, f: F)\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut Self, T),\n    {\n        let mut computation = FlagComputation::new();\n\n        if !value.bound_vars().is_empty() {\n            computation.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_BINDER_VARS);\n        }\n\n        f(&mut computation, value.skip_binder());\n\n        self.add_flags(computation.flags);\n\n        // The types that contributed to `computation` occurred within\n        // a region binder, so subtract one from the region depth\n        // within when adding the depth to `self`.\n        let outer_exclusive_binder = computation.outer_exclusive_binder;\n        if outer_exclusive_binder > ty::INNERMOST {\n            self.add_exclusive_binder(outer_exclusive_binder.shifted_out(1));\n        } // otherwise, this binder captures nothing\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    fn add_kind(&mut self, kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) {\n        match *kind {\n            ty::Bool\n            | ty::Char\n            | ty::Int(_)\n            | ty::Float(_)\n            | ty::Uint(_)\n            | ty::Never\n            | ty::Str\n            | ty::Foreign(..) => {}\n\n            ty::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n\n            ty::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM);\n            }\n\n            ty::Closure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineClosure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineWitness(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Coroutine(_, args) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_CORO);\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Placeholder(..) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n\n            ty::Infer(infer) => match infer {\n                ty::FreshTy(_) | ty::FreshIntTy(_) | ty::FreshFloatTy(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FRESH)\n                }\n\n                ty::TyVar(_) | ty::IntVar(_) | ty::FloatVar(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER)\n                }\n            },\n\n            ty::Adt(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Alias(kind, data) => {\n                self.add_flags(match kind {\n                    ty::Projection => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PROJECTION,\n                    ty::Free => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS,\n                    ty::Opaque => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_OPAQUE,\n                    ty::Inherent => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INHERENT,\n                });\n\n                self.add_alias_ty(data);\n            }\n\n            ty::Dynamic(obj, r) => {\n                for predicate in obj.iter() {\n                    self.bound_computation(predicate, |computation, predicate| match predicate {\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => {\n                            computation.add_args(tr.args.as_slice())\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(p) => {\n                            computation.add_existential_projection(&p);\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_) => {}\n                    });\n                }\n\n                self.add_region(r);\n            }\n\n            ty::Array(tt, len) => {\n                self.add_ty(tt);\n                self.add_const(len);\n            }\n\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_ty_pat(pat);\n            }\n\n            ty::Slice(tt) => self.add_ty(tt),\n\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, _) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Ref(r, ty, _) => {\n                self.add_region(r);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Tuple(types) => {\n                self.add_tys(types);\n            }\n\n            ty::FnDef(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, _) => self.bound_computation(sig_tys, |computation, sig_tys| {\n                computation.add_tys(sig_tys.inputs_and_output);\n            }),\n\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(bound_ty) => {\n                self.bound_computation(bound_ty.into(), |computation, ty| {\n                    computation.add_ty(ty);\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty_pat(&mut self, pat: <I as Interner>::Pat) {\n        self.add_flags(pat.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(pat.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate(&mut self, binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) {\n        self.bound_computation(binder, |computation, atom| computation.add_predicate_atom(atom));\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate_atom(&mut self, atom: ty::PredicateKind<I>) {\n        match atom {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred)) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_pred.trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(ty::HostEffectPredicate {\n                trait_ref,\n                constness: _,\n            })) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                a,\n                b,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_region(a);\n                self.add_region(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                ty,\n                region,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_region(region);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty)) => {\n                self.add_const(ct);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(ty::ProjectionPredicate {\n                projection_term,\n                term,\n            })) => {\n                self.add_alias_term(projection_term);\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term)) => {\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_def_id) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(uv)) => {\n                self.add_const(uv);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(expected, found) => {\n                self.add_const(expected);\n                self.add_const(found);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(ty::NormalizesTo { alias, term }) => {\n                self.add_alias_term(alias);\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, _) => {\n                self.add_term(t1);\n                self.add_term(t2);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_sym)) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty(&mut self, ty: I::Ty) {\n        self.add_flags(ty.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(ty.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_tys(&mut self, tys: I::Tys) {\n        for ty in tys.iter() {\n            self.add_ty(ty);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_region(&mut self, r: I::Region) {\n        self.add_flags(r.flags());\n        if let ty::ReBound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) = r.kind() {\n            self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_const(&mut self, c: I::Const) {\n        self.add_flags(c.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(c.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_const_kind(&mut self, c: &ty::ConstKind<I>) {\n        match *c {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.add_args(uv.args.as_slice());\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PROJECTION);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(infer) => match infer {\n                ty::InferConst::Fresh(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_FRESH),\n                ty::InferConst::Var(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER),\n            },\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => self.add_ty(cv.ty()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(e) => self.add_args(e.args().as_slice()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_existential_projection(&mut self, projection: &ty::ExistentialProjection<I>) {\n        self.add_args(projection.args.as_slice());\n        match projection.term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_alias_ty(&mut self, alias_ty: ty::AliasTy<I>) {\n        self.add_args(alias_ty.args.as_slice());\n    }\n\n    fn add_alias_term(&mut self, alias_term: ty::AliasTerm<I>) {\n        self.add_args(alias_term.args.as_slice());\n    }\n\n    fn add_args(&mut self, args: &[I::GenericArg]) {\n        for arg in args {\n            match arg.kind() {\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(lt) => self.add_region(lt),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_term(&mut self, term: I::Term) {\n        match term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":391,"oldLines":9,"newStart":391,"newLines":9,"lines":[" tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse self::combine::{PredicateEmittingRelation, super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse crate::data_structures::DelayedSet;\npub use crate::relate::*;\nuse crate::solve::Goal;\nuse crate::{self as ty, InferCtxtLike, Interner};\n\npub trait RelateExt: InferCtxtLike {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx: InferCtxtLike> RelateExt for Infcx {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate =\n            SolverRelating::new(self, StructurallyRelateAliases::No, variance, param_env, span);\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":197,"oldLines":10,"newStart":197,"newLines":10,"lines":["                         self.goals.push(Goal::new("," sub_ty: b,\n                                super_ty: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));","originalFile":"use tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse self::combine::{PredicateEmittingRelation, super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse crate::data_structures::DelayedSet;\npub use crate::relate::*;\nuse crate::solve::Goal;\nuse crate::{self as ty, InferCtxtLike, Interner};\n\npub trait RelateExt: InferCtxtLike {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx: InferCtxtLike> RelateExt for Infcx {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate =\n            SolverRelating::new(self, StructurallyRelateAliases::No, variance, param_env, span);\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":210,"oldLines":10,"newStart":210,"newLines":10,"lines":["                         self.goals.push(Goal::new("," rustc_middle::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::combine::{super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":138,"oldLines":10,"newStart":138,"newLines":10,"lines":["                             self.cx(),","                             self.trace.cause.clone(),","
