TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness(trait_pred, constness))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, Lift)]\npub struct PrintClosureAsImpl<'tcx> {\n    pub closure: ty::ClosureArgs<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n}\n\nmacro_rules! forward_display_to_print {\n    ($($ty:ty),+) => {\n        // Some of the $ty arguments may not actually use 'tcx\n        $(#[allow(unused_lifetimes)] impl<'tcx> fmt::Display for $ty {\n            fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n                ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n                    let mut p = FmtPrinter::new(tcx, Namespace::TypeNS);\n                    tcx.lift(*self)\n                        .expect(\"could not lift for printing\")\n                        .print(&mut p)?;\n                    f.write_str(&p.into_buffer())?;\n                    Ok(())\n                })\n            }\n        })+\n    };\n}\n\nmacro_rules! define_print {\n    (($self:ident, $p:ident): $($ty:ty $print:block)+) => {\n        $(impl<'tcx, P: PrettyPrinter<'tcx>> Print<'tcx, P> for $ty {\n            fn print(&$self, $p: &mut P) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n                let _: () = $print;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        })+\n    };\n}\n\nmacro_rules! define_print_and_forward_display {\n    (($self:ident, $p:ident): $($ty:ty $print:block)+) => {\n        define_print!(($self, $p): $($ty $print)*);\n        forward_display_to_print!($($ty),+);\n    };\n}\n\nforward_display_to_print! {\n    ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    Ty<'tcx>,\n    &'tcx ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>,\n    ty::Const<'tcx>\n}\n\ndefine_print! {\n    (self, p):\n\n    ty::FnSig<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.safety.prefix_str())?;\n\n        if self.abi != ExternAbi::Rust {\n            write!(p, \"extern {} \", self.abi)?;\n        }\n\n        write!(p, \"fn\")?;\n        p.pretty_print_fn_sig(self.inputs(), self.c_variadic, self.output())?;\n    }\n\n    ty::TraitRef<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"<{} as {}>\", self.self_ty(), self.print_only_trait_path())?;\n    }\n\n    ty::AliasTy<'tcx> {\n        let alias_term: ty::AliasTerm<'tcx> = (*self).into();\n        alias_term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::AliasTerm<'tcx> {\n        match self.kind(p.tcx()) {\n            ty::AliasTermKind::InherentTy | ty::AliasTermKind::InherentConst => p.pretty_print_inherent_projection(*self)?,\n            ty::AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => {\n                if !(p.should_print_verbose() || with_reduced_queries())\n                    && p.tcx().is_impl_trait_in_trait(self.def_id)\n                {\n                    p.pretty_print_rpitit(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n                } else {\n                    p.print_def_path(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n                }\n            }\n            ty::AliasTermKind::FreeTy\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::FreeConst\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => {\n                p.print_def_path(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::TraitPredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": \")?;\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx> {\n        let constness = match self.constness {\n            ty::BoundConstness::Const => { \"const\" }\n            ty::BoundConstness::Maybe => { \"[const]\" }\n        };\n        self.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": {constness} \")?;\n        self.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::TypeAndMut<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.mutbl.prefix_str())?;\n        self.ty.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ClauseKind<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::ClauseKind::Trait(ref data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::Projection(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                ct.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` has type `\")?;\n                ty.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"`\")?;\n            },\n            ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term) => {\n                term.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" well-formed\")?;\n            }\n            ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(ct) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                ct.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` can be evaluated\")?;\n            }\n            ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(symbol) => {\n                write!(p, \"feature({symbol}) is enabled\")?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::PredicateKind<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(trait_def_id) => {\n                write!(p, \"the trait `\")?;\n                p.print_def_path(trait_def_id, &[])?;\n                write!(p, \"` is dyn-compatible\")?;\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(c1, c2) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                c1.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` equals `\")?;\n                c2.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"`\")?;\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => write!(p, \"ambiguous\")?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, dir) => {\n                t1.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" {dir} \")?;\n                t2.print(p)?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(x) => x.print(p)?,\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(x) => x.print(p)?,\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(def_id) => p.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?,\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> {\n        // Use a type that can't appear in defaults of type parameters.\n        let dummy_self = Ty::new_fresh(p.tcx(), 0);\n        let trait_ref = self.with_self_ty(p.tcx(), dummy_self);\n        trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialProjection<'tcx> {\n        let name = p.tcx().associated_item(self.def_id).name();\n        // The args don't contain the self ty (as it has been erased) but the corresp.\n        // generics do as the trait always has a self ty param. We need to offset.\n        let args = &self.args[p.tcx().generics_of(self.def_id).parent_count - 1..];\n        p.print_path_with_generic_args(|p| write!(p, \"{name}\"), args)?;\n        write!(p, \" = \")?;\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ProjectionPredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.projection_term.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" == \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::SubtypePredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" -> \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::NormalizesTo<'tcx> {\n        self.alias.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" normalizes-to \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n}\n\ndefine_print_and_forward_display! {\n    (self, p):\n\n    &'tcx ty::List<Ty<'tcx>> {\n        write!(p, \"{{\")?;\n        p.comma_sep(self.iter())?;\n        write!(p, \"}}\")?;\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx> {\n        p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, self.0.args)?;\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx> {\n        if !with_reduced_queries()\n            && p.tcx().trait_def(self.0.def_id).paren_sugar\n            && let ty::Tuple(args) = self.0.args.type_at(1).kind()\n        {\n            write!(p, \"{}(\", p.tcx().item_name(self.0.def_id))?;\n            for (i, arg) in args.iter().enumerate() {\n                if i > 0 {\n                    write!(p, \", \")?;\n                }\n                arg.print(p)?;\n            }\n            write!(p, \")\")?;\n        } else {\n            p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, self.0.args)?;\n        }\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName<'tcx> {\n        p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, &[])?;\n    }\n\n    TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx> {\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.0.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.0.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness<'tcx> {\n        self.0.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": \")?;\n        if let Some(constness) = self.1 {\n            p.pretty_print_bound_constness(constness)?;\n        }\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.0.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.0.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    PrintClosureAsImpl<'tcx> {\n        p.pretty_print_closure_as_impl(self.closure)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ParamTy {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.name)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::PlaceholderType {\n        match self.bound.kind {\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Anon => write!(p, \"{self:?}\")?,\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Param(def_id) => match p.should_print_verbose() {\n                true => write!(p, \"{self:?}\")?,\n                false => write!(p, \"{}\", p.tcx().item_name(def_id))?,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ParamConst {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.name)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::Term<'tcx> {\n      match self.kind() {\n        ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => ty.print(p)?,\n        ty::TermKind::Const(c) => c.print(p)?,\n      }\n    }\n\n    ty::Predicate<'tcx> {\n        self.kind().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::Clause<'tcx> {\n        self.kind().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        match self.kind() {\n            GenericArgKind::Lifetime(lt) => lt.print(p)?,\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty.print(p)?,\n            GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => ct.print(p)?,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn for_each_def(tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, mut collect_fn: impl for<'b> FnMut(&'b Ident, Namespace, DefId)) {\n    // Iterate all (non-anonymous) local crate items no matter where they are defined.\n    for id in tcx.hir_free_items() {\n        if matches!(tcx.def_kind(id.owner_id), DefKind::Use) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let item = tcx.hir_item(id);\n        let Some(ident) = item.kind.ident() else { continue };\n\n        let def_id = item.owner_id.to_def_id();\n        let ns = tcx.def_kind(def_id).ns().unwrap_or(Namespace::TypeNS);\n        collect_fn(&ident, ns, def_id);\n    }\n\n    // Now take care of extern crate items.\n    let queue = &mut Vec::new();\n    let mut seen_defs: DefIdSet = Default::default();\n\n    for &cnum in tcx.crates(()).iter() {\n        // Ignore crates that are not direct dependencies.\n        match tcx.extern_crate(cnum) {\n            None => continue,\n            Some(extern_crate) => {\n                if !extern_crate.is_direct() {\n                    continue;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        queue.push(cnum.as_def_id());\n    }\n\n    // Iterate external crate defs but be mindful about visibility\n    while let Some(def) = queue.pop() {\n        for child in tcx.module_children(def).iter() {\n            if !child.vis.is_public() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            match child.res {\n                def::Res::Def(DefKind::AssocTy, _) => {}\n                def::Res::Def(DefKind::TyAlias, _) => {}\n                def::Res::Def(defkind, def_id) => {\n                    if let Some(ns) = defkind.ns() {\n                        collect_fn(&child.ident, ns, def_id);\n                    }\n\n                    if defkind.is_module_like() && seen_defs.insert(def_id) {\n                        queue.push(def_id);\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The purpose of this function is to collect public symbols names that are unique across all\n/// crates in the build. Later, when printing about types we can use those names instead of the\n/// full exported path to them.\n///\n/// So essentially, if a symbol name can only be imported from one place for a type, and as\n/// long as it was not glob-imported anywhere in the current crate, we can trim its printed\n/// path and print only the name.\n///\n/// This has wide implications on error messages with types, for example, shortening\n/// `std::vec::Vec` to just `Vec`, as long as there is no other `Vec` importable anywhere.\n///\n/// The implementation uses similar import discovery logic to that of 'use' suggestions.\n///\n/// See also [`with_no_trimmed_paths!`].\n// this is pub to be able to intra-doc-link it\npub fn trimmed_def_paths(tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, (): ()) -> DefIdMap<Symbol> {\n    // Trimming paths is expensive and not optimized, since we expect it to only be used for error\n    // reporting. Record the fact that we did it, so we can abort if we later found it was\n    // unnecessary.\n    //\n    // The `rustc_middle::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths` wrapper can be used to suppress this\n    // checking, in exchange for full paths being formatted.\n    tcx.sess.record_trimmed_def_paths();\n\n    // Once constructed, unique namespace+symbol pairs will have a `Some(_)` entry, while\n    // non-unique pairs will have a `None` entry.\n    let unique_symbols_rev: &mut FxIndexMap<(Namespace, Symbol), Option<DefId>> =\n        &mut FxIndexMap::default();\n\n    for symbol_set in tcx.resolutions(()).glob_map.values() {\n        for symbol in symbol_set {\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::TypeNS, *symbol), None);\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::ValueNS, *symbol), None);\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::MacroNS, *symbol), None);\n        }\n    }\n\n    for_each_def(tcx, |ident, ns, def_id| match unique_symbols_rev.entry((ns, ident.name)) {\n        IndexEntry::Occupied(mut v) => match v.get() {\n            None => {}\n            Some(existing) => {\n                if *existing != def_id {\n                    v.insert(None);\n                }\n            }\n        },\n        IndexEntry::Vacant(v) => {\n            v.insert(Some(def_id));\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Put the symbol from all the unique namespace+symbol pairs into `map`.\n    let mut map: DefIdMap<Symbol> = Default::default();\n    for ((_, symbol), opt_def_id) in unique_symbols_rev.drain(..) {\n        use std::collections::hash_map::Entry::{Occupied, Vacant};\n\n        if let Some(def_id) = opt_def_id {\n            match map.entry(def_id) {\n                Occupied(mut v) => {\n                    // A single DefId can be known under multiple names (e.g.,\n                    // with a `pub use ... as ...;`). We need to ensure that the\n                    // name placed in this map is chosen deterministically, so\n                    // if we find multiple names (`symbol`) resolving to the\n                    // same `def_id`, we prefer the lexicographically smallest\n                    // name.\n                    //\n                    // Any stable ordering would be fine here though.\n                    if *v.get() != symbol && v.get().as_str() > symbol.as_str() {\n                        v.insert(symbol);\n                    }\n                }\n                Vacant(v) => {\n                    v.insert(symbol);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    map\n}\n\npub fn provide(providers: &mut Providers) {\n    *providers = Providers { trimmed_def_paths, ..*providers };\n}\n\npub struct OpaqueFnEntry<'tcx> {\n    kind: ty::ClosureKind,\n    return_ty: Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, Term<'tcx>>>,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":3254,"oldLines":11,"newStart":3254,"newLines":11,"lines":["         self.term.print(p)?;","     }"," ","-    ty::SubtypePredicate<'tcx> {","-        self.a.print(p)?;","+    ty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {","+        self.sub_ty.print(p)?;","         write!(p, \" <: \")?;","         p.reset_type_limit();","-        self.b.print(p)?;","+        self.super_ty.print(p)?;","     }"," ","     ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Conversion of internal Rust compiler `ty` items to stable ones.\n\nuse rustc_middle::ty::Ty;\nuse rustc_middle::{bug, mir, ty};\nuse rustc_public_bridge::Tables;\nuse rustc_public_bridge::context::CompilerCtxt;\n\nuse crate::alloc;\nuse crate::compiler_interface::BridgeTys;\nuse crate::ty::{\n    AdtKind, FloatTy, GenericArgs, GenericParamDef, IntTy, Region, RigidTy, TyKind, UintTy,\n};\nuse crate::unstable::Stable;\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::AliasTyKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::AliasKind;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::Projection => crate::ty::AliasKind::Projection,\n            ty::Inherent => crate::ty::AliasKind::Inherent,\n            ty::Opaque => crate::ty::AliasKind::Opaque,\n            ty::Free => crate::ty::AliasKind::Free,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::AliasTy<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::AliasTy;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ty::AliasTy { args, def_id, .. } = self;\n        crate::ty::AliasTy { def_id: tables.alias_def(*def_id), args: args.stable(tables, cx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::AliasTerm<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::AliasTerm;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ty::AliasTerm { args, def_id, .. } = self;\n        crate::ty::AliasTerm { def_id: tables.alias_def(*def_id), args: args.stable(tables, cx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::ExistentialPredicate;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ExistentialPredicate::*;\n        match self {\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(existential_trait_ref) => {\n                Trait(existential_trait_ref.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(existential_projection) => {\n                Projection(existential_projection.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(def_id) => AutoTrait(tables.trait_def(*def_id)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::ExistentialTraitRef;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ty::ExistentialTraitRef { def_id, args, .. } = self;\n        crate::ty::ExistentialTraitRef {\n            def_id: tables.trait_def(*def_id),\n            generic_args: args.stable(tables, cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TermKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TermKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::TermKind;\n        match self {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => TermKind::Type(ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(cnst) => {\n                let cnst = cnst.stable(tables, cx);\n                TermKind::Const(cnst)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ExistentialProjection<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::ExistentialProjection;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ty::ExistentialProjection { def_id, args, term, .. } = self;\n        crate::ty::ExistentialProjection {\n            def_id: tables.trait_def(*def_id),\n            generic_args: args.stable(tables, cx),\n            term: term.kind().stable(tables, cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::adjustment::PointerCoercion {\n    type T = crate::mir::PointerCoercion;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use rustc_middle::ty::adjustment::PointerCoercion;\n        match self {\n            PointerCoercion::ReifyFnPointer(safety) => {\n                crate::mir::PointerCoercion::ReifyFnPointer(safety.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PointerCoercion::UnsafeFnPointer => crate::mir::PointerCoercion::UnsafeFnPointer,\n            PointerCoercion::ClosureFnPointer(safety) => {\n                crate::mir::PointerCoercion::ClosureFnPointer(safety.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PointerCoercion::MutToConstPointer => crate::mir::PointerCoercion::MutToConstPointer,\n            PointerCoercion::ArrayToPointer => crate::mir::PointerCoercion::ArrayToPointer,\n            PointerCoercion::Unsize => crate::mir::PointerCoercion::Unsize,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::UserTypeAnnotationIndex {\n    type T = usize;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        self.as_usize()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::AdtKind {\n    type T = AdtKind;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::AdtKind::Struct => AdtKind::Struct,\n            ty::AdtKind::Union => AdtKind::Union,\n            ty::AdtKind::Enum => AdtKind::Enum,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::FieldDef {\n    type T = crate::ty::FieldDef;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        crate::ty::FieldDef {\n            def: tables.create_def_id(self.did),\n            name: self.name.stable(tables, cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::GenericArgs<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::GenericArgs;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        GenericArgs(self.iter().map(|arg| arg.kind().stable(tables, cx)).collect())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::GenericArgKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::GenericArgKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::GenericArgKind;\n        match self {\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(region) => {\n                GenericArgKind::Lifetime(region.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => GenericArgKind::Type(ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Const(cnst) => GenericArgKind::Const(cnst.stable(tables, cx)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, S, V> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Binder<'tcx, S>\nwhere\n    S: Stable<'tcx, T = V>,\n{\n    type T = crate::ty::Binder<V>;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::Binder;\n\n        Binder {\n            value: self.as_ref().skip_binder().stable(tables, cx),\n            bound_vars: self\n                .bound_vars()\n                .iter()\n                .map(|bound_var| bound_var.stable(tables, cx))\n                .collect(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, S, V> Stable<'tcx> for ty::EarlyBinder<'tcx, S>\nwhere\n    S: Stable<'tcx, T = V>,\n{\n    type T = crate::ty::EarlyBinder<V>;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::EarlyBinder;\n\n        EarlyBinder { value: self.as_ref().skip_binder().stable(tables, cx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::FnSig<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::FnSig;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::FnSig;\n\n        FnSig {\n            inputs_and_output: self\n                .inputs_and_output\n                .iter()\n                .map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                .collect(),\n            c_variadic: self.c_variadic,\n            safety: self.safety.stable(tables, cx),\n            abi: self.abi.stable(tables, cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundTyKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::BoundTyKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::BoundTyKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Anon => BoundTyKind::Anon,\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Param(def_id) => {\n                BoundTyKind::Param(tables.param_def(*def_id), cx.tcx.item_name(*def_id).to_string())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundRegionKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::BoundRegionKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::BoundRegionKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::Anon => BoundRegionKind::BrAnon,\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::Named(def_id) => BoundRegionKind::BrNamed(\n                tables.br_named_def(*def_id),\n                cx.tcx.item_name(*def_id).to_string(),\n            ),\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::ClosureEnv => BoundRegionKind::BrEnv,\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::NamedAnon(_) => bug!(\"only used for pretty printing\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundVariableKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::BoundVariableKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::BoundVariableKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Ty(bound_ty_kind) => {\n                BoundVariableKind::Ty(bound_ty_kind.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Region(bound_region_kind) => {\n                BoundVariableKind::Region(bound_region_kind.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Const => BoundVariableKind::Const,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::IntTy {\n    type T = IntTy;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::IntTy::Isize => IntTy::Isize,\n            ty::IntTy::I8 => IntTy::I8,\n            ty::IntTy::I16 => IntTy::I16,\n            ty::IntTy::I32 => IntTy::I32,\n            ty::IntTy::I64 => IntTy::I64,\n            ty::IntTy::I128 => IntTy::I128,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::UintTy {\n    type T = UintTy;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::UintTy::Usize => UintTy::Usize,\n            ty::UintTy::U8 => UintTy::U8,\n            ty::UintTy::U16 => UintTy::U16,\n            ty::UintTy::U32 => UintTy::U32,\n            ty::UintTy::U64 => UintTy::U64,\n            ty::UintTy::U128 => UintTy::U128,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::FloatTy {\n    type T = FloatTy;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::FloatTy::F16 => FloatTy::F16,\n            ty::FloatTy::F32 => FloatTy::F32,\n            ty::FloatTy::F64 => FloatTy::F64,\n            ty::FloatTy::F128 => FloatTy::F128,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for Ty<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Ty;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        tables.intern_ty(cx.lift(*self).unwrap())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TyKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyKind;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::Bool => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool),\n            ty::Char => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char),\n            ty::Int(int_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(int_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Uint(uint_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Uint(uint_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Float(float_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(float_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Adt(adt_def, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(\n                tables.adt_def(adt_def.did()),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Foreign(def_id) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Foreign(tables.foreign_def(*def_id))),\n            ty::Str => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str),\n            ty::Array(ty, constant) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(ty.stable(tables, cx), constant.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Pat(ty.stable(tables, cx), pat.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Slice(ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, mutbl) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(ty.stable(tables, cx), mutbl.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Ref(region, ty, mutbl) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n                ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                mutbl.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnDef(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(\n                tables.fn_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, hdr) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig_tys.with(*hdr).stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            // FIXME(unsafe_binders):\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(_) => todo!(),\n            ty::Dynamic(existential_predicates, region) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(\n                existential_predicates\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|existential_predicate| existential_predicate.stable(tables, cx))\n                    .collect(),\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Closure(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(\n                tables.closure_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::CoroutineClosure(..) => todo!(\"FIXME(async_closures): Lower these to SMIR\"),\n            ty::Coroutine(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(\n                tables.coroutine_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Never => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Never),\n            ty::Tuple(fields) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(\n                fields.iter().map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx)).collect(),\n            )),\n            ty::Alias(alias_kind, alias_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Alias(alias_kind.stable(tables, cx), alias_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::Param(param_ty) => TyKind::Param(param_ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                unreachable!()\n            }\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn_idx), bound_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Bound(debruijn_idx.as_usize(), bound_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::CoroutineWitness(def_id, args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::CoroutineWitness(\n                tables.coroutine_witness_def(*def_id),\n                args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Placeholder(..) | ty::Infer(_) | ty::Error(_) => {\n                unreachable!();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Pattern<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Pattern;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match **self {\n            ty::PatternKind::Range { start, end } => crate::ty::Pattern::Range {\n                // FIXME(SMIR): update data structures to not have an Option here anymore\n                start: Some(start.stable(tables, cx)),\n                end: Some(end.stable(tables, cx)),\n                include_end: true,\n            },\n            ty::PatternKind::NotNull => todo!(),\n            ty::PatternKind::Or(_) => todo!(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Const<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyConst;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ct = cx.lift(*self).unwrap();\n        let kind = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => {\n                let const_val = cx.valtree_to_const_val(cv);\n                if matches!(const_val, mir::ConstValue::ZeroSized) {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::ZSTValue(cv.ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                } else {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::Value(\n                        cv.ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                        alloc::new_allocation(cv.ty, const_val, tables, cx),\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(param) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Param(param.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Unevaluated(\n                tables.const_def(uv.def),\n                uv.args.stable(tables, cx),\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(),\n        };\n        let id = tables.intern_ty_const(ct);\n        crate::ty::TyConst::new(kind, id)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ParamConst {\n    type T = crate::ty::ParamConst;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ParamConst;\n        ParamConst { index: self.index, name: self.name.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ParamTy {\n    type T = crate::ty::ParamTy;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ParamTy;\n        ParamTy { index: self.index, name: self.name.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundTy {\n    type T = crate::ty::BoundTy;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::BoundTy;\n        BoundTy { var: self.var.as_usize(), kind: self.kind.stable(tables, cx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::trait_def::TraitSpecializationKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::TraitSpecializationKind;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::TraitSpecializationKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::trait_def::TraitSpecializationKind::None => TraitSpecializationKind::None,\n            ty::trait_def::TraitSpecializationKind::Marker => TraitSpecializationKind::Marker,\n            ty::trait_def::TraitSpecializationKind::AlwaysApplicable => {\n                TraitSpecializationKind::AlwaysApplicable\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TraitDef {\n    type T = crate::ty::TraitDecl;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::opaque;\n        use crate::ty::TraitDecl;\n\n        TraitDecl {\n            def_id: tables.trait_def(self.def_id),\n            safety: self.safety.stable(tables, cx),\n            paren_sugar: self.paren_sugar,\n            has_auto_impl: self.has_auto_impl,\n            is_marker: self.is_marker,\n            is_coinductive: self.is_coinductive,\n            skip_array_during_method_dispatch: self.skip_array_during_method_dispatch,\n            skip_boxed_slice_during_method_dispatch: self.skip_boxed_slice_during_method_dispatch,\n            specialization_kind: self.specialization_kind.stable(tables, cx),\n            must_implement_one_of: self\n                .must_implement_one_of\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|idents| idents.iter().map(|ident| opaque(ident)).collect()),\n            implement_via_object: self.implement_via_object,\n            deny_explicit_impl: self.deny_explicit_impl,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TraitRef<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TraitRef;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::TraitRef;\n\n        TraitRef::try_new(tables.trait_def(self.def_id), self.args.stable(tables, cx)).unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Generics {\n    type T = crate::ty::Generics;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::Generics;\n\n        let params: Vec<_> = self.own_params.iter().map(|param| param.stable(tables, cx)).collect();\n        let param_def_id_to_index =\n            params.iter().map(|param| (param.def_id, param.index)).collect();\n\n        Generics {\n            parent: self.parent.map(|did| tables.generic_def(did)),\n            parent_count: self.parent_count,\n            params,\n            param_def_id_to_index,\n            has_self: self.has_self,\n            has_late_bound_regions: self\n                .has_late_bound_regions\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|late_bound_regions| late_bound_regions.stable(tables, cx)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for rustc_middle::ty::GenericParamDefKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::GenericParamDefKind;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::GenericParamDefKind;\n        match *self {\n            ty::GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime => GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime,\n            ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { has_default, GenericParamDefKind::Const { has_default }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for rustc_middle::ty::GenericParamDef {\n    type T = crate::ty::GenericParamDef;\n\n    fn ExistentialProjection<'db>>;\npub type ArgOutlivesPredicate<'db> = OutlivesPredicate<'db, GenericArg<'db>>;\n\n/// Compares via an ordering that will not change if modules are reordered or other changes are\n/// made to the tree. In particular, this ordering is preserved across incremental compilations.\nfn stable_cmp_existential_predicate<'db>(\n    a: &ExistentialPredicate<'db>,\n    b: &ExistentialPredicate<'db>,\n) -> Ordering {\n    // FIXME: this is actual unstable - see impl in predicate.rs in `rustc_middle`\n    match (a, b) {\n        (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => Ordering::Equal,\n        (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_a), ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_b)) => {\n            // Should sort by def path hash\n            Ordering::Equal\n        }\n        (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_a), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_b)) => {\n            // Should sort by def path hash\n            Ordering::Equal\n        }\n        (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\n        (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => Ordering::Greater,\n        (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\n        (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_), _) => Ordering::Greater,\n    }\n}\ninterned_vec_db!(BoundExistentialPredicates, BoundExistentialPredicate);\n\nimpl<'db> rustc_type_ir::inherent::BoundExistentialPredicates<DbInterner<'db>>\n    for BoundExistentialPredicates<'db>\n{\n    fn principal_def_id(self) -> Option<TraitIdWrapper> {\n        self.principal().map(|trait_ref| trait_ref.skip_binder().def_id)\n    }\n\n    fn principal(\n        self,\n    ) -> Option<\n        rustc_type_ir::Binder<DbInterner<'db>, rustc_type_ir::ExistentialTraitRef<DbInterner<'db>>>,\n    > {\n        self.inner()[0]\n            .map_bound(|this| match this {\n                ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => Some(tr),\n                _ => None,\n            })\n            .transpose()\n    }\n\n    fn auto_traits(self) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = TraitIdWrapper> {\n        self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| match predicate.skip_binder() {\n            ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => Some(did),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn projection_bounds(\n        self,\n    ) -> impl IntoIterator<\n        Item = rustc_type_ir::Binder<\n            DbInterner<'db>,\n            rustc_type_ir::ExistentialProjection<DbInterner<'db>>,\n        >,\n    > {\n        self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| {\n            predicate\n                .map_bound(|pred| match pred {\n                    ExistentialPredicate::Projection(projection) => Some(projection),\n                    _ => None,\n                })\n                .transpose()\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> rustc_type_ir::relate::Relate<DbInterner<'db>> for BoundExistentialPredicates<'db> {\n    fn relate<R: rustc_type_ir::relate::TypeRelation<DbInterner<'db>>>(\n        relation: &mut R,\n        a: Self,\n        b: Self,\n    ) -> rustc_type_ir::relate::RelateResult<DbInterner<'db>, Self> {\n        let interner = relation.cx();\n\n        // We need to perform this deduplication as we sometimes generate duplicate projections in `a`.\n        let mut a_v: Vec<_> = a.into_iter().collect();\n        let mut b_v: Vec<_> = b.into_iter().collect();\n        // `skip_binder` here is okay because `stable_cmp` doesn't look at binders\n        a_v.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            stable_cmp_existential_predicate(a.as_ref().skip_binder(), b.as_ref().skip_binder())\n        });\n        a_v.dedup();\n        b_v.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            stable_cmp_existential_predicate(a.as_ref().skip_binder(), b.as_ref().skip_binder())\n        });\n        b_v.dedup();\n        if a_v.len() != b_v.len() {\n            return Err(TypeError::ExistentialMismatch(ExpectedFound::new(a, b)));\n        }\n\n        let v = std::iter::zip(a_v, b_v).map(\n            |(ep_a, ep_b): (\n                Binder<'_, ty::ExistentialPredicate<_>>,\n                Binder<'_, ty::ExistentialPredicate<_>>,\n            )| {\n                match (ep_a.skip_binder(), ep_b.skip_binder()) {\n                    (ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(a), ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(b)) => {\n                        Ok(ep_a.rebind(ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(\n                            relation.relate(ep_a.rebind(a), ep_b.rebind(b))?.skip_binder(),\n                        )))\n                    }\n                    (\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(a),\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(b),\n                    ) => Ok(ep_a.rebind(ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(\n                        relation.relate(ep_a.rebind(a), ep_b.rebind(b))?.skip_binder(),\n                    ))),\n                    (\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(a),\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(b),\n                    ) if a == b => Ok(ep_a.rebind(ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(a))),\n                    _ => Err(TypeError::ExistentialMismatch(ExpectedFound::new(a, b))),\n                }\n            },\n        );\n\n        CollectAndApply::collect_and_apply(v, |g| {\n            BoundExistentialPredicates::new_from_iter(interner, g.iter().cloned())\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[salsa::interned(constructor = new_)]\npub struct Predicate<'db> {\n    #[returns(ref)]\n    kind_: InternedWrapperNoDebug<WithCachedTypeInfo<Binder<'db, PredicateKind<'db>>>>,\n}\n\nimpl<'db> std::fmt::Debug for Predicate<'db> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        self.inner().internee.fmt(f)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> std::fmt::Debug\n    for InternedWrapperNoDebug<WithCachedTypeInfo<Binder<'db, PredicateKind<'db>>>>\n{\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"Binder<\")?;\n        match self.0.internee.skip_binder() {\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::Clause(clause_kind) => {\n                write!(f, \"{clause_kind:?}\")\n            }\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(trait_def_id) => {\n                write!(f, \"the trait `{trait_def_id:?}` is dyn-compatible\")\n            }\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::Subtype(subtype_predicate) => {\n                write!(f, \"{subtype_predicate:?}\")\n            }\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::Coerce(coerce_predicate) => {\n                write!(f, \"{coerce_predicate:?}\")\n            }\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(c1, c2) => {\n                write!(f, \"the constant `{c1:?}` equals `{c2:?}`\")\n            }\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => write!(f, \"ambiguous\"),\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(data) => write!(f, \"{data:?}\"),\n            rustc_type_ir::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, dir) => {\n                write!(f, \"{t1:?} {dir:?} {t2:?}\")\n            }\n        }?;\n        write!(f, \", [{:?}]>\", self.0.internee.bound_vars())?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> Predicate<'db> {\n    pub fn new(interner: DbInterner<'db>, kind: Binder<'db, PredicateKind<'db>>) -> Self {\n        let flags = FlagComputation::for_predicate(kind);\n        let cached = WithCachedTypeInfo {\n            internee: kind,\n            flags: flags.flags,\n            outer_exclusive_binder: flags.outer_exclusive_binder,\n        };\n        Predicate::new_(interner.db(), InternedWrapperNoDebug(cached))\n    }\n\n    pub fn inner(&self) -> &WithCachedTypeInfo<Binder<'db, PredicateKind<'db>>> {\n        crate::with_attached_db(|db| {\n            let inner = &self.kind_(db).0;\n            // SAFETY: The caller already has access to a `Predicate<'db>`, so borrowchecking will\n            // make sure that our returned value is valid for the lifetime `'db`.\n            unsafe { std::mem::transmute(inner) }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Flips the polarity of a Predicate.\n    ///\n    /// Given `T: Trait` predicate it returns `T: !Trait` and given `T: !Trait` returns `T: Trait`.\n    pub fn flip_polarity(self) -> Option<Predicate<'db>> {\n        let kind = self\n            .kind()\n            .map_bound(|kind| match kind {\n                PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\n                    trait_ref,\n                    polarity,\n                })) => Some(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\n                    trait_ref,\n                    polarity: polarity.flip(),\n                }))),\n\n                _ => None,\n            })\n            .transpose()?;\n\n        Some(Predicate::new(DbInterner::conjure(), kind))\n    }\n}\n\n// FIXME: should make a \"header\" in interned_vec\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct InternedClausesWrapper<'db>(SmallVec<[Clause<'db>; 2]>, TypeFlags, DebruijnIndex);\n\nimpl<'db> PartialEq for InternedClausesWrapper<'db> {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        self.0.eq(&other.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> Eq for InternedClausesWrapper<'db> {}\n\nimpl<'db> std::hash::Hash for InternedClausesWrapper<'db> {\n    fn hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) {\n        self.0.hash(state)\n    }\n}\n\n#[salsa::interned(constructor = new_)]\npub struct Clauses<'db> {\n    #[returns(ref)]\n    inner_: InternedClausesWrapper<'db>,\n}\n\nimpl<'db> Clauses<'db> {\n    pub fn new_from_iter(\n        interner: DbInterner<'db>,\n        data: impl IntoIterator<Item = Clause<'db>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let clauses: SmallVec<_> = data.into_iter().collect();\n        let flags = FlagComputation::<DbInterner<'db>>::for_clauses(&clauses);\n        let wrapper = InternedClausesWrapper(clauses, flags.flags, flags.outer_exclusive_binder);\n        Clauses::new_(interner.db(), wrapper)\n    }\n\n    pub fn inner(&self) -> &InternedClausesWrapper<'db> {\n        crate::with_attached_db(|db| {\n            let inner = self.inner_(db);\n            // SAFETY: The caller already has access to a `Clauses<'db>`, so borrowchecking will\n            // make sure that our returned value is valid for the lifetime `'db`.\n            unsafe { std::mem::transmute(inner) }\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> std::fmt::Debug for Clauses<'db> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> rustc_type_ir::TypeSuperFoldable<DbInterner<'db>> for Clauses<'db> {\n    fn try_super_fold_with<F: rustc_type_ir::FallibleTypeFolder<DbInterner<'db>>>(\n        self,\n        folder: &mut F,\n    ) -> Result<Self, F::Error> {\n        let mut clauses: SmallVec<[_; 2]> = SmallVec::with_capacity(self.inner().0.len());\n        for c in self {\n            clauses.push(c.try_fold_with(folder)?);\n        }\n        Ok(Clauses::new_from_iter(folder.cx(), clauses))\n    }\n\n    fn super_fold_with<F: rustc_type_ir::TypeFolder<DbInterner<'db>>>(\n        self,\n        folder: &mut F,\n    ) -> bounds which would cause us to\n    /// unsoundly accept some programs. See #91068.\n    fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\n        // TODO: this should probably live in rustc_type_ir\n        match self.inner().as_ref().skip_binder() {\n            PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::WellFormed(_))\n            | PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..)\n            | PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..) => false,\n            PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(_))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(_))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(_))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(_))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(..))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::HostEffect(..))\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_))\n            | PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_)\n            | PredicateKind::Subtype(_)\n            | PredicateKind::Coerce(_)\n            | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(_))\n            | PredicateKind::ConstEquate(_, _)\n            | PredicateKind::Ambiguous => true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'db> Predicate<'db> {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01X6kTriBuJEkQdwk8i1HXv4","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /workspace/src/tools/rust-analyzer/crates/hir-ty/src/infer/fallback.rs has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b9101dfc-ea82-492b-b096-ec2964250a6c","uuid":"ab6aeb10-44f3-47ef-9444-41ba69a6e428","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/workspace/src/tools/rust-analyzer/crates/hir-ty/src/infer/fallback.rs","oldString":"                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    PredicateKind::Coerce(CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    _ => return None,\n                };","newString":"                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    PredicateKind::Coerce(CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty }) => (sub_ty, super_ty),\n                    _ => return None,\n                };","originalFile":"//! Fallback of infer vars to `!` and `i32`/`f64`.\n\nuse intern::sym;\nuse petgraph::{\n    Graph,\n    visit::{Dfs, Walker},\n};\nuse rustc_hash::{FxBuildHasher, FxHashMap, FxHashSet};\nuse rustc_type_ir::{\n    TyVid,\n    inherent::{IntoKind, Ty as _},\n};\nuse tracing::debug;\n\nuse crate::{\n    infer::InferenceContext,\n    next_solver::{CoercePredicate, PredicateKind, SubtypePredicate, Ty, TyKind},\n};\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum DivergingFallbackBehavior {\n    /// Always fallback to `()` (aka \"always spontaneous decay\")\n    ToUnit,\n    /// Sometimes fallback to `!`, but mainly fallback to `()` so that most of the crates are not broken.\n    ContextDependent,\n    /// Always fallback to `!` (which should be equivalent to never falling back + not making\n    /// never-to-any coercions unless necessary)\n    ToNever,\n}\n\nimpl<'db> InferenceContext<'_, 'db> {\n    pub(super) fn type_inference_fallback(&mut self) {\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback start obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.table.fulfillment_cx.pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        // All type checking constraints were added, try to fallback unsolved variables.\n        self.table.select_obligations_where_possible();\n\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback post selection obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.table.fulfillment_cx.pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        let fallback_occurred = self.fallback_types();\n\n        if !fallback_occurred {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // We now see if we can make progress. This might cause us to\n        // unify inference variables for opaque types, since we may\n        // have unified some other type variables during the first\n        // phase of fallback. This means that we only replace\n        // inference variables with their underlying opaque types as a\n        // last resort.\n        //\n        // In code like this:\n        //\n        // ```rust\n        // type MyType = impl Copy;\n        // fn produce() -> MyType { true }\n        // fn bad_produce() -> MyType { panic!() }\n        // ```\n        //\n        // we want to unify the opaque inference variable in `bad_produce`\n        // with the diverging fallback for `panic!` (e.g. `()` or `!`).\n        // This will produce a nice error message about conflicting concrete\n        // types for `MyType`.\n        //\n        // If we had tried to fallback the opaque inference variable to `MyType`,\n        // we will generate a confusing type-check error that does not explicitly\n        // refer to opaque types.\n        self.table.select_obligations_where_possible();\n    }\n\n    fn diverging_fallback_behavior(&self) -> DivergingFallbackBehavior {\n        if self.krate().data(self.db).edition.at_least_2024() {\n            return DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToNever;\n        }\n\n        if self.resolver.def_map().is_unstable_feature_enabled(&sym::never_type_fallback) {\n            return DivergingFallbackBehavior::ContextDependent;\n        }\n\n        DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit\n    }\n\n    fn fallback_types(&mut self) -> bool {\n        // Check if we have any unresolved variables. If not, no need for fallback.\n        let unresolved_variables = self.table.infer_ctxt.unresolved_variables();\n\n        if unresolved_variables.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let diverging_fallback_behavior = self.diverging_fallback_behavior();\n\n        let diverging_fallback =\n            self.calculate_diverging_fallback(&unresolved_variables, diverging_fallback_behavior);\n\n        // We do fallback in two passes, to try to generate\n        // better error messages.\n        // The first time, we do *not* replace opaque types.\n        let mut fallback_occurred = false;\n        for ty in unresolved_variables {\n            debug!(\"unsolved_variable = {:?}\", ty);\n            fallback_occurred |= self.fallback_if_possible(ty, &diverging_fallback);\n        }\n\n        fallback_occurred\n    }\n\n    // Tries to apply a fallback to `ty` if it is an unsolved variable.\n    //\n    // - Unconstrained ints are replaced with `i32`.\n    //\n    // - Unconstrained floats are replaced with `f64`.\n    //\n    // - Non-numerics may get replaced with `()` or `!`, depending on\n    //   how they were categorized by `calculate_diverging_fallback`\n    //   (and the setting of `#![feature(never_type_fallback)]`).\n    //\n    // Fallback becomes very dubious if we have encountered\n    // type-checking errors. In that case, fallback to Error.\n    //\n    // Sets `FnCtxt::fallback_has_occurred` if fallback is performed\n    // during this call.\n    fn fallback_if_possible(\n        &mut self,\n        ty: Ty<'db>,\n        diverging_fallback: &FxHashMap<Ty<'db>, Ty<'db>>,\n    ) -> bool {\n        // Careful: we do NOT shallow-resolve `ty`. We know that `ty`\n        // is an unsolved variable, and we determine its fallback\n        // based solely on how it was created, not what other type\n        // variables it may have been unified with since then.\n        //\n        // The reason this matters is that other attempts at fallback\n        // may (in principle) conflict with this fallback, and we wish\n        // to generate a type error in that case. (However, this\n        // actually isn't true right now, because we're only using the\n        // builtin fallback rules. This would be true if we were using\n        // user-supplied fallbacks. But it's still useful to write the\n        // code to detect bugs.)\n        //\n        // (Note though that if we have a general type variable `?T`\n        // that is then unified with an integer type variable `?I`\n        // that ultimately never gets resolved to a special integral\n        // type, `?T` is not considered unsolved, but `?I` is. The\n        // same is true for float variables.)\n        let fallback = match ty.kind() {\n            TyKind::Infer(rustc_type_ir::IntVar(_)) => self.types.i32,\n            TyKind::Infer(rustc_type_ir::FloatVar(_)) => self.types.f64,\n            _ => match diverging_fallback.get(&ty) {\n                Some(&fallback_ty) => fallback_ty,\n                None => return false,\n            },\n        };\n        debug!(\"fallback_if_possible(ty={:?}): defaulting to `{:?}`\", ty, fallback);\n\n        _ = self.demand_eqtype_fixme_no_diag(ty, fallback);\n        true\n    }\n\n    /// The \"diverging fallback\" system is rather complicated. This is\n    /// a result of our need to balance 'do the right thing' with\n    /// backwards compatibility.\n    ///\n    /// \"Diverging\" type variables are variables created when we\n    /// coerce a `!` type into an unbound type variable `?X`. If they\n    /// never wind up being constrained, the \"right and natural\" thing\n    /// is that `?X` should \"fallback\" to `!`. This means that e.g. an\n    /// expression like `Some(return)` will ultimately wind up with a\n    /// type like `Option<!>` (presuming it is not assigned or\n    /// constrained to have some other type).\n    ///\n    /// However, the fallback used to be `()` (before the `!` type was\n    /// added). Moreover, there are cases where the `!` type 'leaks\n    /// out' from dead code into type variables that affect live\n    /// code. The most common case is something like this:\n    ///\n    /// ```rust\n    /// # fn foo() -> i32 { 4 }\n    /// match foo() {\n    ///     22 => Default::default(), // call this type `?D`\n    ///     _ => return, // return has type `!`\n    /// } // call the type of this match `?M`\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// Here, coercing the type `!` into `?M` will create a diverging\n    /// type variable `?X` where `?X <: ?M`. We also have that `?D <:\n    /// ?M`. If `?M` winds up unconstrained, then `?X` will\n    /// fallback. If it falls back to `!`, then all the type variables\n    /// will wind up equal to `!` -- this includes the type `?D`\n    /// (since `!` doesn't implement `Default`, we wind up a \"trait\n    /// not implemented\" error in code like this). But since the\n    /// original fallback was `()`, this code used to compile with `?D\n    /// = ()`. This is somewhat surprising, since `Default::default()`\n    /// on its own would give an error because the types are\n    /// insufficiently constrained.\n    ///\n    /// Our solution to this dilemma is to modify diverging variables\n    /// so that they can *either* fallback to `!` (the default) or to\n    /// `()` (the backwards compatibility case). We decide which\n    /// fallback to use based on whether there is a coercion pattern\n    /// like this:\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore (not-rust)\n    /// ?Diverging -> ?V\n    /// ?NonDiverging -> ?V\n    /// ?V != ?NonDiverging\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// Here `?Diverging` represents some diverging type variable and\n    /// `?NonDiverging` represents some non-diverging type\n    /// variable. `?V` can be any type variable (diverging or not), so\n    /// long as it is not equal to `?NonDiverging`.\n    ///\n    /// Intuitively, what we are looking for is a case where a\n    /// \"non-diverging\" type variable (like `?M` in our example above)\n    /// is coerced *into* some variable `?V` that would otherwise\n    /// fallback to `!`. In that case, we make `?V` fallback to `!`,\n    /// along with anything that would flow into `?V`.\n    ///\n    /// The algorithm we use:\n    /// * Identify all variables that are coerced *into* by a\n    ///   diverging variable. Do this by iterating over each\n    ///   diverging, unsolved variable and finding all variables\n    ///   reachable from there. Call that set `D`.\n    /// * Walk over all unsolved, non-diverging variables, and find\n    ///   any variable that has an edge into `D`.\n    fn calculate_diverging_fallback(\n        &self,\n        unresolved_variables: &[Ty<'db>],\n        behavior: DivergingFallbackBehavior,\n    ) -> FxHashMap<Ty<'db>, Ty<'db>> {\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback({:?})\", unresolved_variables);\n\n        // Construct a coercion graph where an edge `A -> B` indicates\n        // a type variable is that is coerced\n        let coercion_graph = self.create_coercion_graph();\n\n        // Extract the unsolved type inference variable vids; note that some\n        // unsolved variables are integer/float variables and are excluded.\n        let unsolved_vids = unresolved_variables.iter().filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid());\n\n        // Compute the diverging root vids D -- that is, the root vid of\n        // those type variables that (a) are the target of a coercion from\n        // a `!` type and (b) have not yet been solved.\n        //\n        // These variables are the ones that are targets for fallback to\n        // either `!` or `()`.\n        let diverging_roots: FxHashSet<TyVid> = self\n            .table\n            .diverging_type_vars\n            .iter()\n            .map(|&ty| self.shallow_resolve(ty))\n            .filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid())\n            .map(|vid| self.table.infer_ctxt.root_var(vid))\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_type_vars={:?}\",\n            self.table.diverging_type_vars\n        );\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_roots={:?}\", diverging_roots);\n\n        // Find all type variables that are reachable from a diverging\n        // type variable. These will typically default to `!`, unless\n        // we find later that they are *also* reachable from some\n        // other type variable outside this set.\n        let mut roots_reachable_from_diverging = Dfs::empty(&coercion_graph);\n        let mut diverging_vids = vec![];\n        let mut non_diverging_vids = vec![];\n        for unsolved_vid in unsolved_vids {\n            let root_vid = self.table.infer_ctxt.root_var(unsolved_vid);\n            debug!(\n                \"calculate_diverging_fallback: unsolved_vid={:?} root_vid={:?} diverges={:?}\",\n                unsolved_vid,\n                root_vid,\n                diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid),\n            );\n            if diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid) {\n                diverging_vids.push(unsolved_vid);\n                roots_reachable_from_diverging.move_to(root_vid.as_u32().into());\n\n                // drain the iterator to visit all nodes reachable from this node\n                while roots_reachable_from_diverging.next(&coercion_graph).is_some() {}\n            } else {\n                non_diverging_vids.push(unsolved_vid);\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: roots_reachable_from_diverging={:?}\",\n            roots_reachable_from_diverging,\n        );\n\n        // Find all type variables N0 that are not reachable from a\n        // diverging variable, and then compute the set reachable from\n        // N0, which we call N. These are the *non-diverging* type\n        // variables. (Note that this set consists of \"root variables\".)\n        let mut roots_reachable_from_non_diverging = Dfs::empty(&coercion_graph);\n        for &non_diverging_vid in &non_diverging_vids {\n            let root_vid = self.table.infer_ctxt.root_var(non_diverging_vid);\n            if roots_reachable_from_diverging.discovered.contains(root_vid.as_usize()) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            roots_reachable_from_non_diverging.move_to(root_vid.as_u32().into());\n            while roots_reachable_from_non_diverging.next(&coercion_graph).is_some() {}\n        }\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: roots_reachable_from_non_diverging={:?}\",\n            roots_reachable_from_non_diverging,\n        );\n\n        debug!(\"obligations: {:#?}\", self.table.fulfillment_cx.pending_obligations());\n\n        // For each diverging variable, figure out whether it can\n        // reach a member of N. If so, it falls back to `()`. Else\n        // `!`.\n        let mut diverging_fallback =\n            FxHashMap::with_capacity_and_hasher(diverging_vids.len(), FxBuildHasher);\n\n        for &diverging_vid in &diverging_vids {\n            let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.interner(), diverging_vid);\n            let root_vid = self.table.infer_ctxt.root_var(diverging_vid);\n            let can_reach_non_diverging = Dfs::new(&coercion_graph, root_vid.as_u32().into())\n                .iter(&coercion_graph)\n                .any(|n| roots_reachable_from_non_diverging.discovered.contains(n.index()));\n\n            let mut fallback_to = |ty| {\n                diverging_fallback.insert(diverging_ty, ty);\n            };\n\n            match behavior {\n                DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit => {\n                    debug!(\"fallback to () - legacy: {:?}\", diverging_vid);\n                    fallback_to(self.types.unit);\n                }\n                DivergingFallbackBehavior::ContextDependent => {\n                    // FIXME: rustc does the following, but given this is only relevant when the unstable\n                    // `never_type_fallback` feature is active, I chose to not port this.\n                    // if found_infer_var_info.self_in_trait && found_infer_var_info.output {\n                    //     // This case falls back to () to ensure that the code pattern in\n                    //     // tests/ui/never_type/fallback-closure-ret.rs continues to\n                    //     // compile when never_type_fallback is enabled.\n                    //     //\n                    //     // This rule is not readily explainable from first principles,\n                    //     // but is rather intended as a patchwork fix to ensure code\n                    //     // which compiles before the stabilization of never type\n                    //     // fallback continues to work.\n                    //     //\n                    //     // Typically this pattern is encountered in a function taking a\n                    //     // closure as a parameter, where the return type of that closure\n                    //     // (checked by `relationship.output`) is expected to implement\n                    //     // some trait (checked by `relationship.self_in_trait`). This\n                    //     // can come up in non-closure cases too, so we do not limit this\n                    //     // rule to specifically `FnOnce`.\n                    //     //\n                    //     // When the closure's body is something like `panic!()`, the\n                    //     // return type would normally be inferred to `!`. However, it\n                    //     // needs to fall back to `()` in order to still compile, as the\n                    //     // trait is specifically implemented for `()` but not `!`.\n                    //     //\n                    //     // For details on the requirements for these relationships to be\n                    //     // set, see the relationship finding module in\n                    //     // compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/traits/relationships.rs.\n                    //     debug!(\"fallback to () - found trait and projection: {:?}\", diverging_vid);\n                    //     fallback_to(self.types.unit);\n                    // }\n                    if can_reach_non_diverging {\n                        debug!(\"fallback to () - reached non-diverging: {:?}\", diverging_vid);\n                        fallback_to(self.types.unit);\n                    } else {\n                        debug!(\"fallback to ! - all diverging: {:?}\", diverging_vid);\n                        fallback_to(self.types.never);\n                    }\n                }\n                DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToNever => {\n                    debug!(\n                        \"fallback to ! - `rustc_never_type_mode = \\\"fallback_to_never\\\")`: {:?}\",\n                        diverging_vid\n                    );\n                    fallback_to(self.types.never);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        diverging_fallback\n    }\n\n    /// Returns a graph whose nodes are (unresolved) inference variables and where\n    /// an edge `?A -> ?B` indicates that the variable `?A` is coerced to `?B`.\n    fn create_coercion_graph(&self) -> Graph<(), ()> {\n        let pending_obligations = self.table.fulfillment_cx.pending_obligations();\n        let pending_obligations_len = pending_obligations.len();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: pending_obligations={:?}\", pending_obligations);\n        let coercion_edges = pending_obligations\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter_map(|obligation| {\n                // The predicates we are looking for look like `Coerce(?A -> ?B)`.\n                // They will have no bound variables.\n                obligation.predicate.kind().no_bound_vars()\n            })\n            .filter_map(|atom| {\n                // We consider both subtyping and coercion to imply 'flow' from\n                // some position in the code `a` to a different position `b`.\n                // This is then used to determine which variables interact with\n                // live code, and as such must fall back to `()` to preserve\n                // soundness.\n                //\n                // In practice currently the two ways that this happens is\n                // coercion and subtyping.\n                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    PredicateKind::Coerce(CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    _ => return None,\n                };\n\n                let a_vid = self.root_vid(a)?;\n                let b_vid = self.root_vid(b)?;\n                Some((a_vid.as_u32(), b_vid.as_u32()))\n            });\n        let num_ty_vars = self.table.infer_ctxt.num_ty_vars();\n        let mut graph = Graph::with_capacity(num_ty_vars, pending_obligations_len);\n        for _ in 0..num_ty_vars {\n            graph.add_node(());\n        }\n        graph.extend_with_edges(coercion_edges);\n        graph\n    }\n\n    /// If `ty` is an unresolved type variable, returns its root vid.\n    fn root_vid(&self, ty: Ty<'db>) -> Option<TyVid> {\n        Some(self.table.infer_ctxt.root_var(self.shallow_resolve(ty).ty_vid()?))\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":415,"oldLines":7,"newStart":415,"newLines":7,"lines":["                 // coercion and subtyping.","                 let (a, b) = match atom {"," Rename struct name: `SubtypePredicate` → `SubtypeRelation`\n### 2. Rename fields:\n   - `a: I::Ty` → `sub_ty: I::Ty` (the subtype)\n   - `b: I::Ty` → `super_ty: I::Ty` (the supertype)\n   - `a_is_expected: bool` → Keep as is (semantic meaning different)\n\n### 3. Update all construction sites to use new field names\n### 4. Update all pattern matching/destructuring to use new field names\n### 5. Update all direct field access (e.g., `self.a` → `self.sub_ty`, `self.b` → `self.super_ty`)\n### 6. Update all type aliases to use new name\n### 7. Update all re-exports to use new name\n### 8. Update all imports to use new name\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n1. Start with the main definition in `rustc_type_ir`\n2. Update the public definition in `rustc_public`\n3. Update type aliases in `rustc_middle` and RA\n4. Update all trait implementations\n5. Update all construction sites\n6. Update all destructuring sites\n7. Update all direct field access sites\n8. Verify compilation\n\n## Analysis\n\nThis refactoring affects:\n- **9 compiler crates**: rustc_type_ir, rustc_public, rustc_middle, rustc_infer, rustc_next_trait_solver, rustc_hir_typeck, rustc_trait_selection, and test/RA code\n- **~20+ files** across the codebase\n- **Semantic change**: The field names now clearly indicate subtype vs supertype relationship\n\nThe change is pervasive but systematic:\n1. Struct definition changes (2 files)\n2. Type alias changes (4 files)\n3. Trait implementation changes (4 files)\n4. Usage site changes (15+ files)\n\nAll references must be updated for compilation to succeed, as Rust's type system ensures exhaustive pattern matching.\n\n## Code Changes\n\n### 1. compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs (Lines 909-924)\n\n**Before:**\n```rust\n/// Encodes that `a` must be a subtype of `b`.
